focus: Final Review + Advancement Exam Preparation — All L1 Accent Skills
focus_ar: المراجعة النهائية + التحضير لامتحان الترقية — كل مهارات النطق في المستوى الأول
arabic_challenge: You've spent 9 weeks building 8 distinct connected-speech skills on top of Level 0's phoneme foundation. This final week mirrors L0's own Week 8 self-assessment structure: no new material, just rigorous, honest review — because the L1→L2 advancement exam's Accent section (blueprint §9.3, Table 45) specifically tests a read passage PLUS spontaneous speech, exactly like this week's Day 7.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 1-2: word stress + schwa foundations, flap T/D

isolation.instructions: Revisit your Week 1 and Week 2 recordings before doing today's drill. Notice how much more automatic these feel now compared to 9 weeks ago.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع تسجيلات الأسبوع ١ و٢ قبل تمرين النهارده. لاحظ قد إيه دي بقت تلقائية أكتر مقارنة بـ ٩ أسابيع فاتت.

sentence_practice: It doesn't matter — the water here is better.
I'll reCORD a new REcord this city has never heard.
Better water, better city, better matter.
daily_drills.record_this: It doesn't matter — the water here is better, and I'll record a new record this city has never heard.
daily_drills.self_check: Compare today's recording to your actual Week 1/2 recordings if you saved them. Is there a clear improvement in ease and speed?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 3-4: consonant-vowel linking, glottal stop T

isolation.instructions: Revisit Week 3 and 4 patterns — linking across word boundaries and the glottal stop before syllabic n.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع أنماط الأسبوع ٣ و٤ — الربط بين حدود الكلمات والوقفة الحنجرية قبل الـ n المقطعية.
practice: pick up, look at, an apple

sentence_practice: Pick up an apple and look at the mountain.
Press the button — I'm certain it's important.
Look at an apple near the mountain button.
daily_drills.record_this: Pick up an apple and look at the mountain — press the button, I'm certain it's important.
daily_drills.self_check: Are linking and glottal stop both still distinct and correct when reviewed together after a week's gap?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review Weeks 5-6: vowel-vowel linking, dropped H

isolation.instructions: Revisit Week 5 and 6 patterns — glide insertion between vowels and dropped H in pronouns/auxiliaries.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع أنماط الأسبوع ٥ و٦ — إدخال الصوت الانتقالي بين حروف العلة وحذف H في الضمائر وأفعال المساعدة.
practice: go out, she asked, I am
tell him, ask her, I have been

sentence_practice: Go out and tell him I have been waiting.
She asked me to ask her again.
I have been meaning to go out and ask her.
daily_drills.record_this: Go out and tell him I have been waiting — she asked me to ask her again, but I have been busy.
daily_drills.self_check: Do the glides and dropped-H patterns both still come through naturally after the gap?

isolation.instructions: Revisit Week 7 and 8 patterns — held consonants at word boundaries and schwa in unstressed syllables.
isolation.instructions_ar: راجع أنماط الأسبوع ٧ و٨ — إمساك الأصوات الساكنة عند حدود الكلمات وصوت الشوا في المقاطع بدون نبر.

sentence_practice: It's a big game — tell me about the family camera.
Some milk and a teacher's advice about the family.
About the big game, my teacher gave some milk to the family.
daily_drills.record_this: It's a big game — tell me about the family camera. Some milk and a teacher's advice about the family made it a good day.
daily_drills.self_check: Are held consonants and reduced schwa syllables both holding up together in this final combined review?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Full mock advancement-exam accent section (read passage + spontaneous speech)

isolation.instructions: Simulate the real L1→L2 advancement exam Accent section exactly as described in the blueprint: read a passage aloud, then speak spontaneously for a similar duration. This is your dress rehearsal.
isolation.instructions_ar: حاكي قسم النطق في امتحان الترقية L1→L2 بالضبط زي ما هو موصوف في الخطة: اقرأ فقرة بصوت عالي، وبعدين اتكلم بعفوية لمدة مماثلة. ده بروفة نهائية.
practice: Part A: read the Day 6 review passage below aloud.
Part B: speak for 60 seconds, unscripted, about your goals for Level 2.

sentence_practice: Part A — read aloud: 'About an hour ago, my big brother asked her about the mountain trip.'
Part B — speak freely: 'Talk about why you want to reach Level 2, and what you're most proud of from Level 1.'
daily_drills.record_this: (Record both Part A — reading — and Part B — spontaneous speech — as two separate clips.)
daily_drills.self_check: Compare your Part A (read) fluency to your Part B (spontaneous) fluency. A gap here is normal — but the smaller the gap, the more ready you are for the real exam.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Comprehensive Review: all 8 L1 accent features combined
challenge_sentences: About an hour ago, I picked up an apple, told him about the big game, and asked her about the mountain.
It doesn't matter — the water here is better, and my family's camera captured a good moment.
Go out, press the button, and tell him I have been certain about this big deal.
daily_drills.record_this: About an hour ago, I picked up an apple, told him about the big game, and asked her about the mountain — it doesn't matter, the water here is better, and my family's camera captured a good moment. Go out, press the button, and tell him I have been certain about this big deal.
daily_drills.self_check: This is the longest, most demanding passage in all of L1. Score yourself using L0's Day-7 scoring bands (90-100 / 70-89 / 50-69 / below 50) across all 8 features combined.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Final Self-Assessment — L1 Accent Completion Checkpoint

test_yourself.instructions: This is your official L1 accent completion checkpoint. Record yourself reading the passage below, then record 60-90 seconds of spontaneous speech about your Level 1 journey overall.
test_yourself.passage: About ten weeks ago, I started Level 1 as a beginner in connected speech. My teacher and I have been working on the water-and-butter flap, the button-and-mountain glottal stop, and linking words like pick up an apple and go out. I have also learned to tell him and ask her naturally, dropping the h, and to say about and camera with a proper schwa instead of a full vowel. It's a big deal, and I'm certain I'm better prepared for Level 2 now than I was before.

sounds_to_check.C-V linking (Wk3): pick up, an apple, go out

scoring_guide.90_100: All 8 features present and mostly automatic in both the read passage and spontaneous speech. Ready for the L1→L2 advancement exam's accent section.
scoring_guide.70_89: Most features present in the read passage; some inconsistency in spontaneous speech. Continue practicing before the exam.
scoring_guide.50_69: Features present individually but not yet integrated — recommend repeating Week 9's integration drills before attempting the exam.
scoring_guide.below_50: Significant gaps remain — recommend repeating the specific weak weeks (identify from Day 1-4 reviews) before requesting the advancement exam.

for_arabic_speakers: This week deliberately mirrors L0's Week 8 structure (self-assessment + extended practice) — the format should feel familiar from your very first level.
The Day 5 mock exam is the most important drill this week — it directly previews the real advancement exam format described in the blueprint's Table 45 (Live Speaking + Accent Evaluation sections).
If your Day 7 score is below 70, it is completely reasonable to spend an extra week reviewing before requesting `!exam` — the system rewards genuine readiness over speed.
supplementary_notes.daily_time: 20-25 minutes this week (review + mock exam recording)
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 (mock exam) and Day 7 (final assessment) and keep both — they are useful evidence of your progress and can be compared against your future L2 recordings.
